templates.name: Supabase (Lite)
templates.description: Supabase with PostgreSQL, REST API, and Studio UI - simplified for easy deployment
templates.compose:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    ports:
      - "{{DB_PORT}}:5432"
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_USER=postgres
      - POSTGRES_DB=postgres
    volumes:
      - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./init-supabase-roles.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/01-init-roles.sql
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres -d postgres"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 60s

  rest:
    image: postgrest/postgrest:v12.2.0
    ports:
      - "{{REST_PORT}}:3000"
    environment:
      - PGRST_DB_URI=postgres://authenticator:{{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}@db:5432/postgres
      - PGRST_DB_SCHEMAS=public,storage,graphql_public
      - PGRST_DB_ANON_ROLE=anon
      - PGRST_JWT_SECRET={{JWT_SECRET}}
      - PGRST_DB_USE_LEGACY_GUCS=false
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

  meta:
    image: supabase/postgres-meta:v0.83.2
    ports:
      - "{{META_PORT}}:8080"
    environment:
      - PG_META_PORT=8080
      - PG_META_DB_HOST=db
      - PG_META_DB_PORT=5432
      - PG_META_DB_NAME=postgres
      - PG_META_DB_USER=postgres
      - PG_META_DB_PASSWORD={{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

  studio:
    image: supabase/studio:20240729-ce42139
    ports:
      - "{{STUDIO_PORT}}:3000"
    environment:
      - STUDIO_PG_META_URL=http://meta:8080
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}
      - DEFAULT_ORGANIZATION_NAME=Default Organization
      - DEFAULT_PROJECT_NAME=Default Project
      - SUPABASE_URL=http://rest:3000
      - SUPABASE_PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:{{REST_PORT}}
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_LOGS=true
    depends_on:
      - meta
      - rest
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  db_data:

envHints.description: PostgreSQL password (use only letters and numbers)
JWT secret for API authentication (min 32 characters)

templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Supabase

## On your VPS

1. Allow the required ports through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{DB_PORT}}/tcp     # PostgreSQL
sudo ufw allow {{REST_PORT}}/tcp   # REST API
sudo ufw allow {{STUDIO_PORT}}/tcp # Supabase Studio
sudo ufw reload
```

## Access Supabase

- Studio UI: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{STUDIO_PORT}}
- REST API: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{REST_PORT}}
- PostgreSQL: YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{DB_PORT}}

## Note

This is a simplified Supabase setup without Auth, Realtime, or Storage services. For full Supabase features, use the official self-hosted guide at https://supabase.com/docs/guides/self-hosting/docker
templates.securityNotes:
- Use strong passwords with only letters and numbers (no special characters)
- JWT_SECRET must be at least 32 characters
- Restrict database port access to trusted IPs only
- Enable SSL/TLS for production via reverse proxy
- This is a lite version - for production use official Supabase self-hosting

templates.name: PostgreSQL
templates.description: PostgreSQL database with persistent storage
templates.compose:
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:5432"
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER={{POSTGRES_USER}}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_DB={{POSTGRES_DB}}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U {{POSTGRES_USER}} -d {{POSTGRES_DB}}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to PostgreSQL

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

2. Verify the port is open:
```bash
sudo ufw status | grep {{PORT}}
```

## From your local machine

Connect using psql:
```bash
psql -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -p {{PORT}} -U {{POSTGRES_USER}} -d {{POSTGRES_DB}}
```

Or use a connection string:
```
postgresql://{{POSTGRES_USER}}:{{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}@YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/{{POSTGRES_DB}}
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Change the default password immediately after deployment
- Consider using SSL/TLS for production databases
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses using UFW or cloud security groups
- Regularly backup your database
- Keep PostgreSQL updated to the latest version
- Use strong passwords with mixed characters
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test the database connection
2. Create additional users if needed
3. Configure backup schedules
4. Set up monitoring and alerts
5. Review and optimize PostgreSQL configuration for your workload
6. Consider setting up replication for high availability

templates.name: MySQL
templates.description: MySQL database with persistent storage
templates.compose:
services:
  mysql:
    image: mysql:8
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:3306"
    environment:
      - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MYSQL_DATABASE={{MYSQL_DATABASE}}
      - MYSQL_USER={{MYSQL_USER}}
      - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{MYSQL_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "mysqladmin ping -h localhost -u root -p$$MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  mysql_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to MySQL

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

2. Verify the port is open:
```bash
sudo ufw status | grep {{PORT}}
```

## From your local machine

Connect using MySQL client:
```bash
mysql -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -P {{PORT}} -u {{MYSQL_USER}} -p{{MYSQL_PASSWORD}} {{MYSQL_DATABASE}}
```

Or use a connection string:
```
mysql://{{MYSQL_USER}}:{{MYSQL_PASSWORD}}@YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/{{MYSQL_DATABASE}}
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Use strong passwords for root and user accounts
- Disable remote root login in production
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses
- Enable SSL/TLS for remote connections
- Regularly update MySQL to the latest version
- Set up automated backups
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test database connection
2. Create additional users with specific privileges
3. Configure automated backup scripts
4. Set up monitoring and alerts
5. Optimize MySQL configuration for your workload
6. Review and apply security best practices

templates.name: Redis
templates.description: Redis cache with optional persistence
templates.compose:
services:
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  redis_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Redis

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

2. Verify the port is open:
```bash
sudo ufw status | grep {{PORT}}
```

## From your local machine

Connect using redis-cli:
```bash
redis-cli -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -p {{PORT}}
```

Or use a connection string:
```
redis://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
```
templates.securityNotes:
- WARNING: This configuration has NO password protection
- Add password protection with requirepass in redis.conf
- Use bind directive to restrict connections to specific IPs
- Enable SSL/TLS for production environments
- Disable dangerous commands in production
- Keep Redis updated to the latest version
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test Redis connection
2. Configure password authentication (strongly recommended)
3. Set up persistence configuration (RDB/AOF)
4. Configure maxmemory and eviction policies
5. Set up monitoring and alerts
6. Consider setting up Redis Sentinel for high availability

templates.name: SurrealDB
templates.description: Multi-model database for real-time applications with SQL-style queries, graph relations, and full-text search
templates.compose:
services:
  surrealdb:
    image: surrealdb/surrealdb:latest
    user: root
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8000"
    volumes:
      - surrealdb_data:/data
    command: start --username {{SURREAL_USER}} --password {{SURREAL_PASS}} surrealkv:/data/database.db
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "surreal", "isready", "--endpoint", "http://localhost:8000"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  surrealdb_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to SurrealDB

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

2. Verify the port is open:
```bash
sudo ufw status | grep {{PORT}}
```

## From your local machine

Connect using the SurrealDB CLI:
```bash
surreal sql --endpoint http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}} --username {{SURREAL_USER}} --password {{SURREAL_PASS}} --namespace test --database test
```

Or use a WebSocket connection string:
```
ws://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/rpc
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Change the default root password immediately after deployment
- Consider using SSL/TLS for production databases via reverse proxy
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses using UFW or cloud security groups
- Create namespace/database-scoped users instead of using root for applications
- Keep SurrealDB updated to the latest version
- Use strong passwords with mixed characters
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test database connection using surreal sql CLI
2. Create namespaces and databases for your applications
3. Define schemas with DEFINE TABLE and DEFINE FIELD
4. Create scoped users with limited permissions
5. Set up monitoring and alerts
6. Configure backup schedules for the data volume

templates.name: MongoDB
templates.description: Document-oriented NoSQL database for modern applications
templates.compose:
services:
  mongodb:
    image: mongo:7
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:27017"
    environment:
      - MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME={{MONGO_USER}}
      - MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{MONGO_PASSWORD}}
      - MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE={{MONGO_DATABASE}}
    volumes:
      - mongodb_data:/data/db
      - mongodb_config:/data/configdb
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping')"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  mongodb_data:
  mongodb_config:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to MongoDB

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## From your local machine

Connect using mongosh:
```bash
mongosh "mongodb://{{MONGO_USER}}:{{MONGO_PASSWORD}}@YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/{{MONGO_DATABASE}}?authSource=admin"
```

Or use a connection string:
```
mongodb://{{MONGO_USER}}:{{MONGO_PASSWORD}}@YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/{{MONGO_DATABASE}}?authSource=admin
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Change the default password immediately after deployment
- Use SSL/TLS for production databases via reverse proxy
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses using UFW
- Create application-specific users instead of using root
- Keep MongoDB updated to the latest version
- Enable authentication (already enabled in this template)
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test database connection using mongosh
2. Create additional databases and users for your applications
3. Set up monitoring and alerts
4. Configure backup schedules for the data volume
5. Consider upgrading to replica set for production (see MongoDB Replica Set template)

templates.name: MongoDB Replica Set
templates.description: Production-ready 3-node replica set with transactions and failover support
templates.compose:
services:
  mongodb-primary:
    image: mongo:7
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:27017"
    entrypoint:
      - bash
      - -c
      - |
          set -e
          KEYFILE_PATH=/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile
          if [ ! -s $$KEYFILE_PATH ]; then
            umask 077
            openssl rand -base64 756 > $$KEYFILE_PATH
            chown mongodb:mongodb $$KEYFILE_PATH
            chmod 0400 $$KEYFILE_PATH
          fi
          exec docker-entrypoint.sh "$$@"
      - --
    command: ["--replSet", "rs0", "--bind_ip_all", "--keyFile", "/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile"]
    environment:
      - MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME={{MONGO_USER}}
      - MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{MONGO_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mongodb_primary_data:/data/db
      - mongodb_keyfile:/etc/mongo-keyfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--quiet", "--username", "{{MONGO_USER}}", "--password", "{{MONGO_PASSWORD}}", "--authenticationDatabase", "admin", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping').ok"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5

  mongodb-secondary1:
    image: mongo:7
    entrypoint:
      - bash
      - -c
      - |
          set -e
          KEYFILE_PATH=/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile
          until [ -s $$KEYFILE_PATH ]; do
            echo "Waiting for MongoDB keyfile..."
            sleep 1
          done
          exec docker-entrypoint.sh "$$@"
      - --
    command: ["--replSet", "rs0", "--bind_ip_all", "--keyFile", "/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile", "--auth"]
    volumes:
      - mongodb_secondary1_data:/data/db
      - mongodb_keyfile:/etc/mongo-keyfile:ro
    depends_on:
      - mongodb-primary
    restart: unless-stopped

  mongodb-secondary2:
    image: mongo:7
    entrypoint:
      - bash
      - -c
      - |
          set -e
          KEYFILE_PATH=/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile
          until [ -s $$KEYFILE_PATH ]; do
            echo "Waiting for MongoDB keyfile..."
            sleep 1
          done
          exec docker-entrypoint.sh "$$@"
      - --
    command: ["--replSet", "rs0", "--bind_ip_all", "--keyFile", "/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile", "--auth"]
    volumes:
      - mongodb_secondary2_data:/data/db
      - mongodb_keyfile:/etc/mongo-keyfile:ro
    depends_on:
      - mongodb-primary
    restart: unless-stopped

  mongodb-init:
    image: mongo:7
    depends_on:
      - mongodb-primary
      - mongodb-secondary1
      - mongodb-secondary2
    restart: "no"
    environment:
      - MONGO_USER={{MONGO_USER}}
      - MONGO_PASSWORD={{MONGO_PASSWORD}}
    entrypoint:
      - bash
      - -lc
      - |
          set -e

          echo "Waiting for MongoDB primary (auth)..."
          until mongosh --quiet mongodb-primary:27017/admin --authenticationDatabase admin --username $$MONGO_USER --password $$MONGO_PASSWORD --eval "db.adminCommand({ ping: 1 }).ok" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 1; do
            sleep 1
          done

          echo "Waiting for MongoDB secondaries..."
          until mongosh --quiet mongodb-secondary1:27017/admin --eval "db.hello().ok" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 1; do
            sleep 1
          done
          until mongosh --quiet mongodb-secondary2:27017/admin --eval "db.hello().ok" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 1; do
            sleep 1
          done

          # Idempotency: if already configured, do nothing.
          if mongosh --quiet mongodb-primary:27017/admin --authenticationDatabase admin --username $$MONGO_USER --password $$MONGO_PASSWORD --eval "db.hello().setName" 2>/dev/null | grep -q rs0; then
            echo "Replica set already initialized."
            exit 0
          fi

          echo "Initializing replica set..."
          mongosh --quiet mongodb-primary:27017/admin --authenticationDatabase admin --username $$MONGO_USER --password $$MONGO_PASSWORD --eval "rs.initiate({_id: 'rs0', members: [{_id: 0, host: 'mongodb-primary:27017'}, {_id: 1, host: 'mongodb-secondary1:27017'}, {_id: 2, host: 'mongodb-secondary2:27017'}]})"

          echo "Waiting for PRIMARY..."
          until mongosh --quiet mongodb-primary:27017/admin --authenticationDatabase admin --username $$MONGO_USER --password $$MONGO_PASSWORD --eval "db.hello().isWritablePrimary ? 1 : 0" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 1; do
            sleep 1
          done

          echo "Replica set initialized."

volumes:
  mongodb_primary_data:
  mongodb_secondary1_data:
  mongodb_secondary2_data:
  mongodb_keyfile:
templates.documentation:
## MongoDB Replica Set

### Auto Initialization

This template includes a `mongodb-init` one-off container that automatically runs `rs.initiate()` once all nodes are reachable.

If you're deploying on top of existing volumes (or if init failed), run it manually:

```bash
docker compose -p PROJECT_NAME exec mongodb-primary mongosh --username {{MONGO_USER}} --password YOUR_PASSWORD --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "rs.initiate({_id: 'rs0', members: [{_id: 0, host: 'mongodb-primary:27017'}, {_id: 1, host: 'mongodb-secondary1:27017'}, {_id: 2, host: 'mongodb-secondary2:27017'}]})"
```

Check replica set status:
```bash
docker compose -p PROJECT_NAME exec mongodb-primary mongosh --username {{MONGO_USER}} --password YOUR_PASSWORD --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "rs.status()"
```

### Notes
- This template generates a shared internal keyfile in the `mongodb_keyfile` volume to enable replica set authentication.
- Do not delete the `mongodb_keyfile` volume unless you intend to wipe and recreate the replica set.

### Benefits of Replica Set
- **Transactions**: Multi-document ACID transactions
- **High Availability**: Automatic failover
- **Change Streams**: Real-time data change notifications
- **Read Scaling**: Distribute reads across secondaries
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to MongoDB Replica Set

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## From your local machine

The template exposes the `mongodb-primary` container on port {{PORT}}. For external clients, use a direct connection (this avoids trying to resolve the internal Docker hostnames in the replica set config):

```bash
mongosh "mongodb://{{MONGO_USER}}:{{MONGO_PASSWORD}}@YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/app?authSource=admin&directConnection=true"
```

For in-cluster connections (from other containers on the same Docker network), use all 3 hosts:

```text
mongodb://{{MONGO_USER}}:{{MONGO_PASSWORD}}@mongodb-primary:27017,mongodb-secondary1:27017,mongodb-secondary2:27017/app?replicaSet=rs0&authSource=admin
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Change the default password immediately after deployment
- Use SSL/TLS for production databases
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses
- Create application-specific users
- Verify replica set initialization (check `mongodb-init` logs or run rs.status())
- Do not delete the mongodb_keyfile volume unless you intend to wipe and recreate the replica set
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Verify replica set status with rs.status() (auto-initialized)
2. Create application users and databases
3. Configure backup schedules
4. Set up monitoring for all nodes

templates.name: Elasticsearch
templates.description: Distributed search and analytics engine (512MB heap for development)
templates.compose:
services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: elasticsearch:8.11.0
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9200"
      - "{{TRANSPORT_PORT}}:9300"
    environment:
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD={{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}}
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -s -u elastic:{{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}} http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q 'green\\|yellow'"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  elasticsearch_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Elasticsearch

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## From your local machine

Test connection:
```bash
curl -u elastic:{{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}} http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
```

Check cluster health:
```bash
curl -u elastic:{{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}} http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/_cluster/health?pretty
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Change the default password immediately after deployment
- Use HTTPS via reverse proxy for production
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses
- Keep Elasticsearch updated
- Monitor memory usage (512MB heap is for development only)
- For production workloads, use the Production template with 4GB+ heap
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test cluster health via API
2. Create indices for your data
3. Set up index templates and mappings
4. Configure ILM policies for data retention
5. Set up monitoring with Kibana
6. For production, upgrade to Elasticsearch Production template

templates.name: Elasticsearch (Production)
templates.description: Production-ready Elasticsearch with 4GB heap (recommended: 50% of RAM up to 32GB)
templates.compose:
services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: elasticsearch:8.11.0
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9200"
      - "{{TRANSPORT_PORT}}:9300"
    environment:
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD={{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}}
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms4g -Xmx4g"
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -s -u elastic:{{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}} http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q 'green\\|yellow'"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  elasticsearch_data:
templates.documentation:
## Production Elasticsearch

### Memory Configuration
- Default heap: 4GB (adjust based on available RAM)
- Recommended: Set heap to 50% of available RAM, maximum 32GB
- Ensure vm.max_map_count=262144 on host

### Host Configuration
On the host machine, run:
```bash
sudo sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144
echo 'vm.max_map_count=262144' | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf
```

### Heap Sizing Guidelines
- 8GB RAM server → 4GB heap (default)
- 16GB RAM server → 8GB heap
- 32GB+ RAM server → 16GB heap (max recommended)
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Elasticsearch

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## Configure vm.max_map_count (required)
```bash
sudo sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144
echo 'vm.max_map_count=262144' | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf
```

## From your local machine

Test connection:
```bash
curl -u elastic:{{ELASTIC_PASSWORD}} http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/_cluster/health?pretty
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Use strong passwords
- Enable HTTPS via reverse proxy
- Restrict network access
- Monitor cluster health and performance
- Set up snapshot repository for backups
- Consider multi-node cluster for high availability
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Configure vm.max_map_count on host
2. Test cluster health
3. Create indices with proper mappings
4. Set up ILM policies
5. Configure snapshot repository for backups
6. Set up monitoring dashboards

templates.name: CouchDB
templates.description: Seamless multi-master sync with built-in REST API
templates.compose:
services:
  couchdb:
    image: couchdb:3
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:5984"
    environment:
      - COUCHDB_USER={{COUCHDB_USER}}
      - COUCHDB_PASSWORD={{COUCHDB_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - couchdb_data:/opt/couchdb/data
      - couchdb_config:/opt/couchdb/etc/local.d
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:5984/"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  couchdb_data:
  couchdb_config:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to CouchDB

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## From your local machine

Access Fauxton UI:
http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/_utils

Test API:
```bash
curl -u {{COUCHDB_USER}}:{{COUCHDB_PASSWORD}} http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/
```

List databases:
```bash
curl -u {{COUCHDB_USER}}:{{COUCHDB_PASSWORD}} http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/_all_dbs
```
templates.securityNotes:
- Change the default password immediately
- Use HTTPS via reverse proxy for production
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses
- Configure per-database security for multi-tenant apps
- Enable SSL for replication between nodes
- Keep CouchDB updated
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access Fauxton UI at http://SERVER:{{PORT}}/_utils
2. Create databases for your applications
3. Set up design documents for views and filters
4. Configure replication if using multiple nodes
5. Set up monitoring
6. Configure backup schedules

templates.name: Cassandra
templates.description: Highly scalable distributed database with authentication enabled
templates.compose:
services:
  cassandra:
    image: cassandra:4.1
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9042"
      - "{{JMX_PORT}}:7199"
    environment:
      - CASSANDRA_CLUSTER_NAME={{CLUSTER_NAME}}
      - CASSANDRA_DC={{DATACENTER}}
      - CASSANDRA_RACK={{RACK}}
      - CASSANDRA_AUTHENTICATOR=PasswordAuthenticator
      - CASSANDRA_AUTHORIZER=CassandraAuthorizer
      - HEAP_NEWSIZE={{HEAP_NEWSIZE}}
      - MAX_HEAP_SIZE={{MAX_HEAP_SIZE}}
    volumes:
      - cassandra_data:/var/lib/cassandra
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "cqlsh -u cassandra -p cassandra -e 'describe cluster' || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 60s

volumes:
  cassandra_data:
templates.documentation:
## Cassandra Authentication

This template has authentication enabled by default using PasswordAuthenticator.

### Default Credentials
- Username: `cassandra`
- Password: `cassandra`

### First Login - Change Default Password

```bash
docker exec -it PROJECT_NAME-cassandra cqlsh -u cassandra -p cassandra
```

Then run:
```sql
ALTER USER cassandra WITH PASSWORD 'your_new_secure_password';
```

### Create Application User

```sql
CREATE USER app_user WITH PASSWORD 'app_password' NOSUPERUSER;
GRANT ALL PERMISSIONS ON KEYSPACE your_keyspace TO app_user;
```
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Cassandra

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## From your local machine

Connect using cqlsh:
```bash
cqlsh YOUR_SERVER_IP {{PORT}} -u cassandra -p YOUR_PASSWORD
```

Or use a driver connection string:
```
cassandra://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
```
templates.securityNotes:
- IMMEDIATELY change the default cassandra/cassandra password after first login
- Create application-specific users with limited permissions
- Use SSL/TLS for production client connections
- Restrict access to specific IP addresses
- Monitor JMX metrics for performance
- Keep Cassandra updated
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Connect with cqlsh using default credentials (cassandra/cassandra)
2. IMMEDIATELY change the default password
3. Create a keyspace for your application
4. Create application-specific users with limited permissions
5. Set up monitoring via JMX
6. Configure backup schedules for the data volume

templates.name: MariaDB
templates.description: Community-developed MySQL fork with enhanced features
templates.compose:
services:
  mariadb:
    image: mariadb:11
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:3306"
    environment:
      - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MARIADB_DATABASE={{DATABASE}}
      - MARIADB_USER={{USER}}
      - MARIADB_PASSWORD={{PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mariadb_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "healthcheck.sh --connect --innodb_initialized"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  mariadb_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: mysql -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -P {{PORT}} -u {{USER}} -p
templates.securityNotes: Use strong passwords and restrict access to trusted IPs
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test connection
2. Create additional users as needed
3. Set up backups

templates.name: TimescaleDB
templates.description: Time-series database built on PostgreSQL for metrics, events, and analytics
templates.compose:
services:
  timescaledb:
    image: timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:5432"
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER={{POSTGRES_USER}}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_DB={{POSTGRES_DB}}
    volumes:
      - timescaledb_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U {{POSTGRES_USER}} -d {{POSTGRES_DB}}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  timescaledb_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: psql -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -p {{PORT}} -U {{POSTGRES_USER}} -d {{POSTGRES_DB}}
templates.securityNotes: Use strong passwords and enable SSL for production
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Enable TimescaleDB extension: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;
2. Create hypertables for time-series data

templates.name: ClickHouse
templates.description: Fast open-source OLAP database for real-time analytics
templates.compose:
services:
  clickhouse:
    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8123"
      - "{{NATIVE_PORT}}:9000"
    environment:
      - CLICKHOUSE_USER={{CLICKHOUSE_USER}}
      - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD={{CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD}}
      - CLICKHOUSE_DEFAULT_ACCESS_MANAGEMENT=1
    volumes:
      - clickhouse_data:/var/lib/clickhouse
      - clickhouse_logs:/var/log/clickhouse-server
    restart: unless-stopped
    ulimits:
      nofile:
        soft: 262144
        hard: 262144

volumes:
  clickhouse_data:
  clickhouse_logs:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
HTTP Interface: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
Native client: clickhouse-client -h YOUR_SERVER_IP --port {{NATIVE_PORT}} -u {{CLICKHOUSE_USER}} --password
templates.securityNotes: Use strong passwords and restrict access. ClickHouse is optimized for analytics, not OLTP.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test connection via HTTP or native client
2. Create databases and tables
3. Set up data ingestion pipelines

templates.name: SQL Server 2019
templates.description: Microsoft SQL Server 2019 Developer Edition
templates.compose:
services:
  mssql:
    image: mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2019-latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:1433"
    environment:
      - ACCEPT_EULA=Y
      - MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD={{SA_PASSWORD}}
      - MSSQL_PID=Developer
    volumes:
      - mssql_data:/var/opt/mssql
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  mssql_data:

envHints.description: SA password (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit)

templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: sqlcmd -S YOUR_SERVER_IP,{{PORT}} -U sa -P 'your_password'
templates.securityNotes: SA password must be strong (8+ chars with uppercase, lowercase, digit). Developer Edition is free but not for production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Connect with sqlcmd or SSMS
2. Create databases and users
3. Set up backups

templates.name: Valkey
templates.description: Open-source Redis fork maintained by Linux Foundation
templates.compose:
services:
  valkey:
    image: valkey/valkey:8-alpine
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:6379"
    volumes:
      - valkey_data:/data
    command: valkey-server --appendonly yes
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "valkey-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  valkey_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: valkey-cli -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -p {{PORT}}
templates.securityNotes: No password by default. Add requirepass for production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test connection
2. Configure password authentication
3. Set up persistence

templates.name: KeyDB
templates.description: High-performance Redis-compatible database with multithreading
templates.compose:
services:
  keydb:
    image: eqalpha/keydb:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:6379"
    volumes:
      - keydb_data:/data
    command: keydb-server --appendonly yes --server-threads 2
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "keydb-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  keydb_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: redis-cli -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -p {{PORT}}
templates.securityNotes: No password by default. Use --requirepass for production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Test connection with redis-cli
2. Configure authentication
3. Tune server-threads based on CPU cores

templates.name: ScyllaDB
templates.description: High-performance Cassandra-compatible NoSQL database
templates.compose:
services:
  scylladb:
    image: scylladb/scylla:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9042"
      - "{{API_PORT}}:10000"
    volumes:
      - scylla_data:/var/lib/scylla
    command: --smp 1 --memory 750M --overprovisioned 1 --api-address 0.0.0.0
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  scylla_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: cqlsh YOUR_SERVER_IP {{PORT}}
templates.securityNotes: Enable authentication in production. Default has no auth.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Connect with cqlsh
2. Create keyspaces and tables
3. Enable authentication

templates.name: FerretDB
templates.description: MongoDB-compatible database built on PostgreSQL
templates.compose:
services:
  ferretdb:
    image: ferretdb/ferretdb:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:27017"
    environment:
      - FERRETDB_POSTGRESQL_URL=postgres://{{POSTGRES_USER}}:{{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}@postgres:5432/{{POSTGRES_DB}}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER={{POSTGRES_USER}}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_DB={{POSTGRES_DB}}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U {{POSTGRES_USER}} -d {{POSTGRES_DB}}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: mongosh mongodb://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/
templates.securityNotes: Data is stored in PostgreSQL. Secure the PostgreSQL backend.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Connect with mongosh or MongoDB drivers
2. Create collections (stored as PostgreSQL tables)

templates.name: InfluxDB
templates.description: Time-series database for metrics, events, and real-time analytics
templates.compose:
services:
  influxdb:
    image: influxdb:2-alpine
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8086"
    environment:
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_MODE=setup
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_USERNAME={{INFLUX_USER}}
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_PASSWORD={{INFLUX_PASSWORD}}
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_ORG={{INFLUX_ORG}}
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_BUCKET={{INFLUX_BUCKET}}
      - DOCKER_INFLUXDB_INIT_ADMIN_TOKEN={{INFLUX_TOKEN}}
    volumes:
      - influxdb_data:/var/lib/influxdb2
      - influxdb_config:/etc/influxdb2
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  influxdb_data:
  influxdb_config:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
UI: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
API: Use INFLUX_TOKEN for authentication
templates.securityNotes: Keep your admin token secure. Create read-only tokens for applications.

templates.name: OpenSearch
templates.description: Open-source search and analytics suite (Elasticsearch fork)
templates.compose:
services:
  opensearch:
    image: opensearchproject/opensearch:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9200"
      - "{{PERF_PORT}}:9600"
    environment:
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - OPENSEARCH_INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
      - "OPENSEARCH_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
    volumes:
      - opensearch_data:/usr/share/opensearch/data
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
    restart: unless-stopped

  opensearch-dashboards:
    image: opensearchproject/opensearch-dashboards:latest
    ports:
      - "{{DASHBOARD_PORT}}:5601"
    environment:
      - OPENSEARCH_HOSTS=["https://opensearch:9200"]
    depends_on:
      - opensearch
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  opensearch_data:

envHints.description: Admin password (8+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit, special)

templates.externalAccessInstructions:
API: https://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}} (user: admin)
Dashboards: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{DASHBOARD_PORT}}
templates.securityNotes: Password must be strong (8+ chars with uppercase, lowercase, digit, special char)

templates.name: Weaviate
templates.description: AI-native vector database for semantic search and RAG applications
templates.compose:
services:
  weaviate:
    image: semitechnologies/weaviate:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8080"
      - "{{GRPC_PORT}}:50051"
    environment:
      - QUERY_DEFAULTS_LIMIT=25
      - AUTHENTICATION_ANONYMOUS_ACCESS_ENABLED=true
      - PERSISTENCE_DATA_PATH=/var/lib/weaviate
      - DEFAULT_VECTORIZER_MODULE=none
      - CLUSTER_HOSTNAME=node1
    volumes:
      - weaviate_data:/var/lib/weaviate
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  weaviate_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
REST API: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
gRPC: YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{GRPC_PORT}}
templates.securityNotes: Anonymous access enabled by default. Configure authentication for production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access REST API
2. Create schema and classes
3. Configure vectorizers if needed

templates.name: Apache Kafka
templates.description: Distributed event streaming platform with KRaft mode (no Zookeeper)
templates.compose:
services:
  kafka:
    image: apache/kafka:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9092"
    environment:
      - KAFKA_NODE_ID=1
      - KAFKA_PROCESS_ROLES=broker,controller
      - KAFKA_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://:9092,CONTROLLER://:9093
      - KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://localhost:{{PORT}}
      - KAFKA_CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES=CONTROLLER
      - KAFKA_LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP=CONTROLLER:PLAINTEXT,PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT
      - KAFKA_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS=1@localhost:9093
      - KAFKA_OFFSETS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1
      - KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1
      - KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_MIN_ISR=1
      - KAFKA_LOG_DIRS=/var/lib/kafka/data
    volumes:
      - kafka_data:/var/lib/kafka/data
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  kafka_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
Bootstrap server: YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
Use Kafka CLI tools or client libraries to connect
templates.securityNotes: No authentication by default. Configure SASL/SSL for production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Create topics
2. Test with kafka-console-producer and kafka-console-consumer
3. Configure retention policies

templates.name: Hydra
templates.description: Postgres-compatible columnar database for fast analytics queries
templates.compose:
services:
  hydra:
    image: ghcr.io/hydradatabase/hydra:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:5432"
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER={{POSTGRES_USER}}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{POSTGRES_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_DB={{POSTGRES_DB}}
    volumes:
      - hydra_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U {{POSTGRES_USER}} -d {{POSTGRES_DB}}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  hydra_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions: Connect: psql -h YOUR_SERVER_IP -p {{PORT}} -U {{POSTGRES_USER}} -d {{POSTGRES_DB}}
templates.securityNotes: Use strong passwords. Hydra is Postgres-compatible so standard Postgres security applies.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Connect with psql or any Postgres client
2. Create columnar tables with USING columnar
3. Load data for analytics

templates.name: Neon Local
templates.description: Postgres-compatible local proxy for Neon serverless Postgres projects
templates.compose:
services:
  neon-local:
    image: neondatabase/neon_local:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:5432"
    restart: unless-stopped

envHints.description: Delete ephemeral branch when Neon Local stops (true/false)

templates.externalAccessInstructions:
Connect using Postgres clients:

postgresql://neon:npg@YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/neondb?sslmode=require
templates.securityNotes:
- Keep NEON_API_KEY secret
- Restrict access to port {{PORT}} to trusted IPs
- Neon Local uses TLS to Neon; keep client SSL mode enabled
- Use DELETE_BRANCH=true for ephemeral preview workflows
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Get NEON_API_KEY and NEON_PROJECT_ID from Neon dashboard
2. Connect with psql or your app using postgres://neon:npg@YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}/neondb?sslmode=require
3. For persistent preview branches, set DELETE_BRANCH=false
